name: The Going Forth
subtitle: Pabbajja
description: You see, as the Bodhisatta saw, that the household life is dusty and constraining. Something in you turns toward freedom. The first step is the hardest — establishing one true practice and showing up for it daily.

name: The Forest of Fear
subtitle: Bhayabherava
description: The Bodhisatta went to remote forests where fear and dread arose in the night. He chose to sit through the fear instead of running from it. As your practice deepens, the easy days end. The shadows grow restless. This is normal. This is the path.

name: The Two Teachers
subtitle: Āḷāra and Uddaka
description: The Bodhisatta studied with the two greatest meditation teachers of his time. He mastered what they taught — and found it was not yet liberation. You will face the temptation of false summits: techniques that feel like progress but stop short. Keep walking.

name: The Middle Way
subtitle: Majjhimā Paṭipadā
description: After years of extreme asceticism, the Bodhisatta nearly died — and remembered the natural calm under the rose-apple tree as a child. He took food. He found the middle way: neither indulgence nor harshness. The sixteen steps of ānāpānasati open before you.

name: Beneath the Bodhi Tree
subtitle: Bodhirukkha
description: The Bodhisatta sat down beneath the Bodhi tree and made his vow: "Let only skin, sinew, and bone remain. Let the flesh and blood dry up. I will not rise from this seat until I have reached awakening." Mara's full army arrives. The final test. Hold the seat.
